channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Trump vows to hit Iran hard, impose Hormuz transit fees
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
US President Trump vows to strike Iran “hard” and impose a 20% transit fee on all cargo through the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating conflict.
Read More: https://punchng.com/trump-vows-to-hit-iran-hard-impose-hormuz-transit-fees/

item.title: Norway receive heroes’ welcome after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Norway’s national football team received a hero’s welcome in Oslo after their historic World Cup quarter-final run. Fans celebrated the team’s success.
Read More: https://punchng.com/norway-receive-heroes-welcome-after-world-cup-exit/

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Stakeholders Urge Dedicated Nutrition Budget In Bauchi LGs
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Local Government Chairmen in Bauchi State have been urged to prioritise domestic funding for nutrition programmes to sustain the progress recorded in improving the health and wellbeing of women and children. The appeal was made by the State Chairperson, Civil Society Scaling Up Nutrition in Nigeria

item.title: I Borrowed N400m To Secure Appointment, My Lenders Have Petitioned EFCC— Adeyemi
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The embattled Director-General of the disowned Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, has claimed that the N400 million he allegedly paid to secure his appointment was borrowed, revealing that his creditors have now petitioned the Economic and Financial Crimes

item.title: White House backs England to win World Cup

item.title: Liverpool latest: Inter Milan make Jones their top midfield target
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: The U.S. is maxing out its strategic oil reserves as Trump vows to control the Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Strategic Petroleum Reserve stockpiles are precariously low and have been hit by major equipment failures, leaks and spills, according to a government report.

item.title: Court Did Not Nullify ADC Primaries, Atiku Insists, Heads To Supreme Court
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Vice President and presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, has dismissed claims that the Court of Appeal nullified the party’s primary elections for the 2027 general elections, describing such reports as “misinformation” aimed at confusing Nigerians an

item.title: Trump reinstates Iran port blockade and vows 20% charge on cargo passing through Hormuz
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US military later said it had started the third consecutive night of strikes on Iran.

item.title: Senate Probes N34trn Import Duty Waivers, Threatens Sanctions For 14 Defaulting MDAs
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Senate Committee on Finance, on Monday, launched an investigation into the Federal Government’s issuance of Import Duty Exemption Certificates (IDECs) valued at about ₦34 trillion, as part of a wider probe into revenue leakages and remittance failures by federal agencies. The committee also issu

item.title: ‎FG Approves 1,200 Take-Off Staff for FUTA Teaching Hospital ‎
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has approved a take-off establishment of 1,200 personnel for the Federal University of Technology Akure Teaching Hospital (FUTATH), marking a major milestone in the transition of the institution into a full-fledged federal teaching hospital. ‎The chief medical director of the

item.title: 'Hero, legend, sweetheart': Tributes to Jurassic Park actor Sam Neill, who has died aged 78
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.

item.title: Violence: Adeleke Advocates Economic Sanctions, Visa Ban On Oyetola, Fadahunsi, Oyebamiji, Others
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Governor Ademola Adeleke has advocated economic sanctions and visa bans on Blue Marine Minister, Gboyega Oyetola; Senator representing Osun East Senatorial District, Francis Fadahunsi; All Progressives Congress (APC) Gubernatorial candidate Asiwaju Bola Oyebamiji; Hon. Wole Oke; and some leaders. Th

item.title: EDITORIAL: Beyond the Oyo rescue: It’s time to make Nigeria’s schools truly safe
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Schools should never become hunting grounds for criminals. Children go to school to learn, not to live in fear. Teachers go to classrooms to build the future of the nation, not to risk being taken hostage. If parents begin to fear sending their children to school, the country's education system will

item.title: Adeleke demands visa ban for APC chieftains, party faults gov
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun Governor Ademola Adeleke seeks economic sanctions and a visa ban on APC chieftains, citing political violence ahead of the 2026 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/adeleke-demands-visa-ban-for-apc-chieftains-party-faults-gov/

item.title: Backed by Trump, Lindsey Graham’s sister Darline will replace him in Senate
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: South Carolina governor chooses Darline Graham Nordone to serve the rest of the late US senator's term until early 2027.

item.title: Nine deaths tied to Trump’s immigration enforcement operations in US
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Fatal shooting in Biddeford, Maine, is latest in at least nine deaths linked to US president's immigration crackdown.

item.description: The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Hon. Benjamin Kalu, has called for deliberate “co-communication” between Nigeria and the European Union (EU), as well as the adoption of near-shoring models to change negative global narratives and boost investor confidence in Nigeria. Seyi Makinde of Oyo State has called for independent scrutiny of the May 14 abduction of children and teachers from Ahoro-Esinle and Yawota communities in Oriire Local Government Area of the state. item.title: Police rescue Oyo headmaster hours after N30m ransom demand
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The police disclosed that the rescued headmaster has been taken to a medical facility for evaluation and necessary treatment.

The appointments, announced on Monday, July 13, 2026, by the Chief Press Secretary to the Gover

item.dc:creator: TVC News

item.title: Import Duty Waivers Hit N34tn In 2025, Customs Tells Senate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Comptroller-General of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), Bashir Adewale Adeniyi, has disclosed that the value of Import Duty Exemption Certificate (IDEC) approvals rose to about N34 trillion in 2025, significantly affecting the service’s revenue generation. Adeniyi made the disclosure on

item.title: Alli Donates Brand-New Car To Kidnapped Oriire School Principal
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship candidate in Oyo State, Senator Sharafadeen Alli, has donated a brand-new Toyota Camry to Mrs Rachael Folake Alamu, the Principal of Community High School, Esiele, Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, in recognition of her courage and resilience

item.title: Why a borrowing binge by investors is a warning sign for the stock market
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Investors are increasingly borrowing to buy stocks, a reflection of greed in the stock market as they seek to amplify returns with margin debt. The growing pile of borrowed money has some on Wall Street nervous.

item.description: President Bola Tinubu, has said the late immediate past president of Nigeria, Muhammadu Buhari gave everything he had to the country without reservation. Tinubu said this at the One Year Remembrance of the former president and unveiling of a complimentary book titled:“Tributes and Condolences in Hon

item.title: Gold Miners Cadillac, Amapa Minerals Add to Canada IPO Rebound
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Cadillac Mines Corp. and Amapa Minerals Holdings Inc. filed for initial public offerings, in the latest sign that the pace of Canadian listings is starting to pick up.

item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Fitch Ratings, in its latest report for July 2026, affirmed Benue State’s Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) at 'B' and the National Long-Term Rating from ‘A-(nga)’ to ‘A(nga)’, with a Stable Outlook. item.title: 'Easy to create divisions' - Kane says England are 'completely together'
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England captain Harry Kane says the squad are "completely together" before their World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The company has shed more than $800 billion in market value from its $2.67 trillion peak in less than a month.

item.title: Oil Holds Gains as Iran Stokes Inflation Worries: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil held gains and Asian stocks were set to track Wall Street lower after the standoff between the US and Iran intensified, raising concerns that disruptions to energy supplies could accelerate inflation.

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The IGP and police authorities have refused to comply with the court's September 2025 judgement ordering them to release the casefile to the businessman's spouse and prosecute some police officers implicated in the man's disappearance. The post Court receives update on discovery of missing casefile

item.title: Oil Holds Gains After Trump Reinstates Blockade on Iranian Ships
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil steadied after its biggest advance since April, as US President Donald Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded payment for all other cargo moving through the waterway.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Benue State chapter of the Miyetti Allah Cattle Breeders Association of Nigeria (MACBAN) has exonerated its members from the recent killings of ten residents in Otukpo-Nobi, Otukpo Local Government Area of the State. item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Oyo State Police Command has confirmed the release of a kidnapped headmaster, Mathew Owoade, after sustained security operations forced his abductors to abandon him. The post Abducted Oyo headmaster regains freedom — Police appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US judge voids Donald Trump 'improper' $1.8 billion settlement with IRS
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A US judge on Monday voided a settlement between President Donald Trump and the Internal Revenue Service over leaked tax returns, ruling the lawsuit was filed for an "improper purpose." The agreement had granted Trump, his family and business broad protections from tax audits following their $10 bil

item.description:

item.title: Microsoft tests Windows Search without all the ads and fluff
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Microsoft is testing a cleaner version of the Windows 11 search menu that strips it of recommended content and ads. In a blog post on Monday, Microsoft announced that it's rolling out the decluttered Search Box to Windows Insiders in the Experimental channel as the company looks to regain trust with

item.title: NCDC condemns Benue attacks, demands swift justice for perpetrators
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The North Central Development Commission (NCDC) has expressed profound grief and concern over the reported attacks on the Otukpo-Nobi and Akpachi-Ugboju communities in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State. The attacks resulted in the tragic loss of lives and the destruction of homes and other

The Federal Ministry of Industry, Trade and

item.title: OnePlus is reportedly bailing on the US
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: OnePlus and its parent company, Oppo, plan to announce in the coming days that OnePlus brand will be leaving the US and European markets, according to a machine translation of a WinFuture report. Should the exit actually happen, it will mark a conclusion to months of rumors about the future of OnePl

Mike Mayo, Head of US Large-Cap Bank Research at W

Joshua and the Solid Miners are yet to agreement on a new contract. The midfielder played a crucial role in the club’s fifth place finish in the NPFL last season. Nasarawa Un

item.title: After Biden Denied Alabama Coal Waste Bid, Trump Reverses Course
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency on Monday proposed to approve Alabama’s application to take over issuing certain coal waste permits from the agency, part of a trend under President Donald Trump to transfer more authority from the federal government to states.

item.dc:creator: Daily Trust

Since assuming office in 2019, the Fintiri administration has recorded remarkable interv

item.title: Nigerian hospital celebrates survival of its smallest preterm baby
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: A baby girl born at 25 weeks and weighing just 750g has been discharged from the hospital after six months of intensive care, marking the facility's smallest surviving newborn and underscoring its growing capacity to manage extremely premature infants. item.description: A crowd of more than 100,000, an open-top bus parade and a royal reception welcomed back the Norwegian World Cup squad.

Work has already commenced on the project, with the

item.description: First Lady of Nigeria, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, has reaffirmed the present administration’s commitment to investment in early childhood development. item.title: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

item.title: World Cup 2026: We’re Not Afraid Of Spain –Rabiot

item.description: AC Milan star Adrien Rabiot has disclosed that the French national team does not fear Spain ahead of their FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final clash. He emphasized that France does not need a special anti-Yamal plan and is solely focused on playing a solid, collective match against a highly dangerous Spa

item.title: Police rescue kidnapped farmer in Oyo
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has announced the rescue of a victim of a recent kidnapping in the state. The command said Mr Owoade Matthew, who was kidnapped on July 13, has been rescued. The announcement was made in a statement signed by the command’s Public Relations Officer, Olayinka Ay

item.title: Man bags 20-Year Jail Term for Shielding Bandit
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Abuja, on Monday, sentenced one Muhammad Sani to 20 years imprisonment for conspiring to conceal information about an alleged notorious bandit, Danranmi, which led to his escape. Sani of Nabi Village in Shiroro Local Government Area of Niger State, was convicted on Co

item.title: Dangote Refinery Resumes Sale Of Petrol In Dollars
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Dangote Petroleum Refinery has switched payment for the sale of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), Automotive Gas Oil (AGO), and Aviation Turbine Kerosene (ATK) to U.S. dollar. The new policy, which took effect immediately, applies to both gantry and coastal product sales. Industry sources familiar with th

item.title: Kidnapped Oyo headmaster regains freedom after 48 hours
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A middle-aged headmaster named Mr Owoade Mathew has regained his freedom after being kidnapped on his farm and held captive for 48 hours. Mr Owoade was abducted on his farmland located on the Igbojaye-Budo Aare farm road in the Itesiwaju local government area of Oyo State at 9:50 AM on Saturday. The

item.title: England to make late call on Rice for World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England will make a late decision on whether Declan Rice is fit to start Wednesday's World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

item.title: Just in: US military begins third night of strikes on Iran
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The US military said Monday it had launched a fresh salvo of attacks on Iran, marking the third night in a row that it had struck the country, as a deal to end the war falters. The post Just in: US military begins third night of strikes on Iran appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Experts raise alarm over threats to water quality in Niger
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Experts have expressed concern over the increasing threats posed by pollution, climate change and other human activities to water resources in Niger State, calling for urgent action to protect water bodies from further degradation. The warning was issued during a sensitisation programme on the prote

item.title: ‘I’m Not Hiding, I Only Fear for My Life,’ Says Adeyemi Amid ‘Presidential Council’ Controversy
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Adeyemi, however, said he was prepared to defend himself in court. The post ‘I’m Not Hiding, I Only Fear for My Life,’ Says Adeyemi Amid ‘Presidential Council’ Controversy appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Championship: Arokodare omitted from Wolves’ Portugal training camp
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Tolu Arokodare has been left out of Wolves’ squad for their pre-season tour of Portugal amid the uncertainty surrounding his future at the club. Arokodare is keen to leave the Molineux this summer following the club’s relegation last season. Wolves rejected a loan offer from Serie A club

item.title: Police in South Africa open probe into post-World Cup death of Jayden Adams
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The country's football players' union confirmed Adams's death on Saturday, but the cause has not been revealed.

item.title: One year after death, Buhari’s life still ‘speaks’ to Nigerians – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: President Tinubu says Mr Buhari’s transformation from military ruler to civilian democrat marked him out as a rare figure in the nation's history. The post One year after death, Buhari’s life still ‘speaks’ to Nigerians – Tinubu appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Late US senator’s sister appointed to fill vacant seat
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Darline Graham Nordone will serve in the US Senate until Graham's term expires in January, following his unexpected death at the weekend at the age of 71. The post Late US senator’s sister appointed to fill vacant seat appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Illegal mining, money laundering fueling insecurity- Reps
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Ndubuisi Orji, Abuja The House of Representatives has identified illegal mining and money laundering as major drivers of insecurity in the country. The chairman, House Ad Hoc Committee on Mineral Exploitation, Security and Anti-Money Laundering, Sanni Abdulraheem, stated this, on Monday at a

item.title: World Cup semi-finals: 8 Arsenal players eying glory [See names]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: At least eight Arsenal players will be playing at the World Cup semi-final stage this week. France will tackle Spain in the first semi-final tie on Tuesday, while Argentina will take on England in the second semi-final fixture on Wednesday. The winners of both fixtures will qualify for the

item.title: Brazil court bars Bolsonaro son from visiting father until after election
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Brazil's top court on Monday barred presidential hopeful Flavio Bolsonaro from visiting his father, the former president Jair Bolsonaro, who is under house arrest, until after the first round of October elections. The post Brazil court bars Bolsonaro son from visiting father until after election app

item.title: Remembrance: Buhari remains an epitome of integrity, modesty in life and death — Buratai
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Former Chief of Army Staff, Lt. Gen. Tukur Yusuf Buratai (rtd), has stated that former President Muhammadu Buhari epitomizes integrity and modesty in both life and death. In a statement issued on Monday to commemorate the one-year anniversary of President Buhari’s passing, Buratai, who previou

item.title: Kano court upholds journalists’ right to protect sources
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Kano has upheld the right of journalists to refuse to disclose their sources of information, except in matters involving a breach of national security or where the information has a direct link to a case before the court. The court also affirmed its power to subpoena

item.title: Haaland, teammates receive heroes’ welcome in Norway after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Erling Haaland's side were knocked out of the tournament on Saturday after losing 2-1 to England in extra-time in the last eight in Miami, their best result in the competition. The post Haaland, teammates receive heroes’ welcome in Norway after World Cup exit appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US, Iran exchange attacks around Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: This is a breaking news story.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Referee Rob Dieperink who was dropped for sexual assault, dies
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Dutch football association, KNVB, announced the sad news in a statement on Monday. “We are shocked and deeply saddened by the passing of referee Rob Dieperink,” the statement partly read “Our thoughts go out to his family, friends, and everyone who held him dear. We wish t

item.title: Reps, Senate set for harmonisation of State Police Bill
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The leadership of the 10th House of Representatives has set up the Conference Committee that would facilitate the harmonisation of the The post Reps, Senate set for harmonisation of State Police Bill appeared first on Tribune Online.
